channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: 2 Minute Medicine Rewind February 9, 2026
item.description: Reproductive Shifts and Ovarian Cancer Risk in Women Aged 40 Years or Older 1. Across the premenopausal and postmenopausal groups, early menarche, lat...

item.title: March for Life attendees may have been exposed to measles, DC Health warns
item.description: D.C. health officials are contacting people possibly exposed to measles at the March for Life in January, as confirmed cases rise nationwide.

item.title: New study finds many tuberculosis diagnoses may be incorrect
item.description: New research is now casting doubt on the true number of tuberculosis cases. That could carry significant implications for patient care and well-being.

item.title: Need a new path in midlife? There's a school for that and a quiz to kickstart it
item.description: Schools across the country are offering courses and retreats for people 50+ who want to reinvent themselves and embrace lifelong learning and discover...

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Simple choices prevent chronic disease [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Internal medicine physician Shirisha Kamidi discusses her article “Sm...

item.title: New study reveals gaps in Apple Watch's ability to detect undiagnosed high blood pressure
item.description: In September 2025, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration cleared the Apple Watch Hypertension Notifications Feature, a cuffless tool that uses the wat...

item.title: Why flu season can strain your heart, and what a new mRNA approach suggests
item.description: Mount Sinai researchers have identified a cellular mechanism linking infections from influenza A viruses (IAVs) to cardiovascular disease, providing c...

item.title: Outdated Medicare rule delays nursing care and wastes hospital resources, study finds
item.description: A long-standing Medicare policy meant to manage rehabilitation services in nursing homes may keep older Americans in hospitals longer than necessary w...

item.title: Benefits of Statins Far Outweigh Side Effect Risks
item.description: A large-scale study finds that most listed statin side effects are minimal, while the cardiovascular benefits dominate, prompting calls to revise labe...

item.title: Consuming 2–3 cups of coffee daily associated with lower dementia risk, better cognitive function
item.description: A new prospective cohort study by investigators from Mass General Brigham, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, and the Broad Institute of MIT a...

item.title: Overlooked group of gut bacteria appears key to good health, global study finds
item.description: In a huge global study led by University of Cambridge researchers, a single group of bacteria—named CAG-170—has repeatedly shown up in high numbers in...

item.title: When language becomes the barrier: IMGs and autism diagnoses
item.description: I once left grand rounds with a donut and a fake pager buzz because I couldn’t understand the speaker, who often was a natural Mandarin or Arabic spea...

item.title: CVD Risk Factor Counseling Value to Cancer Survivors Unclear
item.description: Researchers aimed to determine whether a survivorship care plan including counseling sessions could improve control of CVD risk factors in adult survi...

item.title: How Indoor and Outdoor Air Quality Affect COPD
item.description: Data support strategies to improve air quality and mitigate increased risk for COPD exacerbations. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Why Med Students Think They’re Dying (When They’re Not)
item.description: Studying disease amid the stress of medical school can make every headache a tumor and every muscle twitch ALS. Welcome to ‘med school syndrome.’ Try ...

item.title: Trump administration lauds plastic surgeons' statement on trans surgery for minors
item.description: A patient who came to regret the top surgery she got as a teen won a $2 million malpractice suit. Then, the American Society of Plastic Surgeons clari...

item.title: Charles Bonnet syndrome: Why the blind see hallucinations
item.description: Today I awoke feeling hopeless, disconnected from my body and my thoughts. All I could sense was the void that my vision loss represented. I kept thin...

item.description: The tension between physicians and the American Board of Internal Medicine (ABIM) has simmered for a decade. What began as a professional debate over ...

item.title: An ultrasound test may more reliably detect ovarian cancer in premenopausal women than the Risk of Malignancy Index
item.description: 1. This cohort study found that the IOTA ADNEX ultrasound model at a 10% risk threshold detected more ovarian cancers in premenopausal women than the ...

item.title: Chimeric antigen receptor T-cell therapy may induce sustained remission in multirefractory autoimmune hemolytic anemia
